children.name: Physics I
children.description: Classical and modern physics foundations: variational principles, Lagrangian and Hamiltonian mechanics, special relativity, quantum-mechanical wave behavior, Schrodinger equation, expectation values, and one-dimensional quantum systems.

children.name: Physics II
children.description: Vector calculus and electromagnetism: electrostatics, magnetostatics, boundary-value methods, dielectric and magnetic media, Faraday induction, Maxwell equations, electromagnetic waves, polarization, reflection, and transmission.

children.name: Physics Lab.
children.description: Laboratory work in first-year physics, emphasizing experimental setup, measurement practice, uncertainty estimation, data analysis, and written reporting across mechanics, electricity, magnetism, optics, and thermal physics modules.

children.name: Mathematical Physics
children.description: Mathematical methods for physics: complex analysis, contour integration and residues, partial differential equations, Green functions, Fourier and Laplace transforms, and introductory group theory including SO(2), SO(3), SU(2), and crystallographic point groups.

children.name: Electromagnetics
children.description: Advanced electromagnetism: electrostatics and boundary-value problems, magnetostatics, magnetic materials, Maxwell equations, electromagnetic energy and momentum, plane waves in media, polarization, reflection and refraction, and waveguide basics.

children.name: Classical Mechanics
children.description: Analytical mechanics through Hamilton principle, generalized coordinates, Lagrange equations, symmetry and conservation laws, central-force motion, rigid-body dynamics, Hamiltonian methods, Poisson brackets, Hamilton-Jacobi theory, and small oscillations.

children.name: Computational Physics
children.description: Scientific computing for physical systems: numerical error and stability, root finding, interpolation, differentiation and integration, linear systems and eigenproblems, ODE and PDE solvers, Monte Carlo methods, and computational modeling examples.

children.name: Heat and Thermodynamics
children.description: Thermodynamics and kinetic theory: state variables, equations of state, first and second laws, thermodynamic potentials, Maxwell relations, entropy, phase transitions, Clausius-Clapeyron relation, transport phenomena, and distribution functions.

children.name: Quantum Mechanics I
children.description: Foundations of quantum mechanics: wave functions, operators, measurement, the Schrodinger equation, probability current, one-dimensional potentials, harmonic oscillator, angular momentum, and introductory three-dimensional central-potential problems.

children.name: Quantum Mechanics II
children.description: Advanced quantum mechanics: time-independent and time-dependent perturbation theory, variation and WKB methods, Heisenberg and interaction pictures, two-level systems, Fermi golden rule, semi-classical radiation theory, scattering theory, and foundational questions.

children.name: Statistical Mechanics
children.description: Probability and statistical mechanics: random walks, binomial, Gaussian and Poisson distributions, central limit theorem, classical ensembles, partition functions, thermodynamic quantities, ideal gases, quantum statistics, black-body radiation, phonons, and fluctuations.

children.name: Solid State Physics
children.description: Solid-state physics: crystal structures, reciprocal lattice, diffraction, lattice vibrations and phonons, free-electron and band theories, semiconductors and metals, density of states, transport, Hall effect, and introductory magnetism.

children.name: Atomic and Molecular Spectroscopy
children.description: Atomic and molecular spectra: hydrogenic atoms, fine structure, Zeeman and Stark effects, selection rules, many-electron atoms, term symbols, rotational and vibrational spectra, electronic transitions, Franck-Condon principle, and spectroscopy applications.

children.name: Lasers and Ultrafast Optics
children.description: Laser and ultrafast optics: Einstein coefficients, population inversion, gain media, optical resonators, modes, Q-switching, mode locking, pulse generation, nonlinear optical effects, second-harmonic generation, and applications in spectroscopy and communications.

children.name: Nuclear Science and Engineering
children.description: Nuclear science and engineering: nuclear structure, radioactive decay, nuclear reactions and cross sections, neutron physics, moderation, reactor criticality, diffusion and kinetics basics, radiation detection, dosimetry, applications, and safety.

children.name: Simulation Techniques in Physical Systems
children.description: Simulation techniques for physical systems: random number generation and testing, sampling, Monte Carlo integration, stochastic processes, Markov chains, random walks, Metropolis algorithm, data correlation and autocorrelation, and molecular dynamics basics.

children.name: Applied Superconductivity
children.description: Applied superconductivity: superconducting materials, London model, flux quantization, type-II behavior, critical fields, pinning, microwave electrodynamics, Josephson junctions, SQUIDs, superconducting electronics, quantum circuits, magnets, and magnetic levitation.

children.name: Quantum Technology and Phenomena in Macroscopic Systems
children.description: Quantum technologies in engineered macroscopic systems: harmonic oscillators, two-level systems, ladder operators, coherent states, Bloch vectors, circuit QED, dissipation and Lindblad master equations, multi-qubit architectures, entanglement, and cavity optomechanics.

children.name: Fundamentals of Cosmology
children.description: Cosmology fundamentals: general relativity review, FLRW metric, Friedmann equations, thermal history, CMB formation, relic abundance, dark matter and dark energy, baryogenesis, structure formation, density perturbations, CMB anisotropies, and inflation.

children.name: Advanced Statistical Mechanics
children.description: Advanced statistical mechanics: phase transitions, critical phenomena, critical exponents, scaling and universality, ensemble methods, Ising, Potts, XY and Heisenberg models, mean-field and Landau theories, transfer matrices, series expansions, and renormalization group ideas.

children.name: Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos
children.description: Nonlinear dynamics and chaos: dynamical systems and state spaces, fixed-point stability, one- and two-dimensional flows, nonlinear oscillators, Poincare-Bendixson theorem, Lorenz-type systems, bifurcations, maps, Lyapunov exponents, routes to chaos, fractals, and attractors.

children.name: Electroceramics
children.description: Electroceramic materials for electrical and electronic applications: ionic structure, defects, polarization, phase transitions, conduction, charge displacement, ceramic processing, sintering, dielectric, ferroelectric, piezoelectric, pyroelectric, electro-optic, and magnetic ceramics.

children.name: General Physics Lab
children.description: General physics laboratory experiments covering mechanics, heat, electricity and magnetism, optics, data acquisition, uncertainty analysis, and clear experimental reporting.

children.name: Advanced Physics Laboratory
children.description: Advanced laboratory experiments in modern physics, optics, lasers, solid state physics, nuclear physics, microwave or electronics methods, with emphasis on instrument use, analysis, calibration, and research-style reporting.

children.name: Mini Project
children.description: Independent or small-team project work in engineering physics, requiring problem definition, literature or design review, implementation or experimentation, analysis, presentation, and a written technical report.

children.name: Measurement Techniques
children.description: Experimental measurement methods: accuracy and precision, error analysis, sensors and transducers, signal conditioning, electrical instruments, noise and filtering, data acquisition, calibration, and practical measurement design.

children.name: Engineering Optics
children.description: Optical systems and wave optics: matrix formulation for lenses and mirrors, stops and pupils, cardinal points, chromatic and monochromatic aberrations, interference, diffraction, polarization, coherence, gratings, and resolving power.

children.name: Basic Electronics
children.description: Introductory electrical and electronics foundations: circuit laws, network theorems, DC and AC circuit analysis, semiconductor devices, diodes, transistors, amplifiers, operational amplifiers, instrumentation basics, and simple electronic systems.

children.name: Basic Electronics Laboratory
children.description: Laboratory experiments for basic electronics, including circuit measurement, diode and transistor characteristics, amplifier and op-amp circuits, logic circuits, instrumentation practice, breadboarding, debugging, and technical reporting.

children.name: Signals and Systems
children.description: Signals and systems: continuous- and discrete-time signals, linear time-invariant systems, convolution, impulse response, Fourier series and transforms, Laplace and Z transforms, sampling, filtering, frequency response, and system stability.

children.name: Semiconductor Devices
children.description: Semiconductor device physics and circuits: energy bands, doping, drift and diffusion, p-n junctions, diode equation and breakdown, rectifiers, BJTs, biasing, small-signal models, FETs, MOS capacitors, threshold voltage, and op-amp basics.

children.name: Digital Electronics and Microprocessors
children.description: Digital electronics and microprocessors: number systems, Boolean algebra, logic minimization, combinational and sequential circuits, flip-flops, counters, registers, memory, microprocessor architecture, instruction sets, addressing modes, assembly programming, interrupts, and I/O interfacing.

children.name: Analog Electronics
children.description: Analog electronics: diode circuits, BJT and FET operation, biasing, amplifier models and frequency response, feedback, oscillators, operational amplifier configurations, filters, integrators, differentiators, and circuit applications.

children.name: Electronics Laboratory - I
children.description: Laboratory experiments in analog and digital electronics, including diode and transistor characteristics, amplifiers, op-amp circuits, filters, oscillators, logic gates, flip-flops, and measurement instrumentation.

children.name: Electronics Lab-II
children.description: Advanced electronics laboratory work covering amplifier design, op-amp applications, filters, oscillators, digital circuits, microprocessor or interfacing modules, instrumentation, circuit debugging, and structured experimental reporting.

children.name: Mathematics I
children.description: First mathematics core: single-variable and multivariable calculus, sequences and series, differential calculus, integral calculus, vector spaces and linear algebra foundations, and mathematical tools used across engineering and science courses.

children.name: Mathematics II
children.description: Second mathematics core: linear algebra, ordinary differential equations, multivariable calculus, transforms, complex variables or probability-oriented mathematical tools, with emphasis on engineering applications and problem solving.

children.name: Real Analysis
children.description: Mathematics minor course in analysis: metric and normed vector spaces, convergence and completeness, pointwise and uniform convergence of function sequences, differentiability of functions of several variables, inverse and implicit function theorems, and introductory Lebesgue measure and integration.

children.name: Mathematical Statistics
children.description: Mathematics minor course in statistics: probability spaces, random variables and vectors, distributions, expectations, convergence, sampling distributions, estimation, sufficiency, maximum likelihood, interval estimation, hypothesis testing, likelihood ratio tests, correlation, and regression.

children.name: Scientific Computing
children.description: Mathematics minor course in scientific computing: numerical errors, nonlinear equation solvers, interpolation and splines, numerical integration, Gaussian quadrature, ODE initial-value methods, multistep and predictor-corrector schemes, finite-difference PDE methods, stability, and convergence.

children.name: Modern Algebra
children.description: Mathematics minor course in algebra: integers, equivalence and congruence, rings, homomorphisms, ideals, domains, fields, groups, cosets, Lagrange theorem, normal subgroups, quotient and permutation groups, group actions, Sylow theorems, PIDs, UFDs, polynomial rings, and field extensions.

children.name: Differential Geometry
children.description: Mathematics minor course in geometry: local theory of plane and space curves, curvature and torsion, Serret-Frenet formulas, regular surfaces, tangent planes, first and second fundamental forms, Gauss map, principal curvatures, isometries, geodesics, and Gauss-Bonnet theorem.

children.name: Introduction to Computing
children.description: Introductory computing and programming: algorithmic thinking, variables, control flow, functions, arrays and data structures, recursion basics, numerical problem solving, debugging, and writing programs for engineering and science applications.

children.name: Computing Lab
children.description: Hands-on programming laboratory accompanying introductory computing, focused on implementing algorithms, debugging, testing, working with input and output, and building confidence with computational problem solving.

children.name: Computer Networks
children.description: Computer networks: network evolution, data-link framing and sliding-window protocols, medium access control, virtual-circuit switching, IP addressing, ARP, ICMP, CIDR, routing algorithms and protocols, UDP, TCP, flow and congestion control, quality of service, DNS, web, email, authentication, and encryption.

children.name: Operating Systems
children.description: Operating systems: processes, threads and scheduling, mutual exclusion, synchronization, semaphores and deadlocks, memory allocation and protection, paging and segmentation, virtual memory, file operations and file-system implementation, free-space and directory management, mounting, device drivers, and disk scheduling.

children.name: Deep Learning
children.description: Deep learning: machine-learning fundamentals, perceptrons, backpropagation, regularization, deep networks, PCA, restricted Boltzmann machines, sparse autoencoders, deep belief networks, hidden Markov models, CNN architectures, activation and pooling layers, vanishing-gradient mitigation, dropout, batch normalization, sequence models, RNNs, LSTMs, GANs, zero-shot learning, and applications.

children.name: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning
children.description: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning (RL); Markov Chains: Discrete-time Markov chains, Stationary Distribution, Continuous-time Markov Chains. Markov Decision Process (MDP): Terminologies & Fundamentals; Finite Horizon MDP: Dynamic Programming (DP), Bellman Equation; Infinite Horizon Discounted Cost Problems: DP Equation, Fixed Point & Contraction Mapping, Value Iteration (VI), Policy Iteration (PI), Linear Programming formulation; RL Techniques: Multi-armed Bandit; Exploration & Exploitation, Optimism in the Face of Uncertainty, Upper Confidence Bound, Thompson Sampling; Monte-Carlo Methods: First-visit and Every-visit, Monte-Carlo Control, Off-policy Monte Carlo Control; Temporal Difference Learning: TD Prediction, Optimality of TD(0), SARSA, Q-learning, n-step TD Prediction, TD (lambda) algorithm, Linear Function Approximation, Linear TD (lambda); Policy Search Methods: Policy Gradient (PG) Theorem, REINFORCE, Variance Reduction in PG Methods, Actor-critic Methods, Batch RL.

children.name: Game Theory and Economics
children.description: Economic game theory: strategic-form games, best responses, Nash equilibrium, mixed strategies, dynamic games, sequential rationality, information structures, repeated games, bargaining or auctions, and applications to economic decision making.

children.name: Economics of Uncertainty and Information
children.description: Economics under uncertainty and information constraints: risk, expected utility, insurance, asymmetric information, adverse selection, moral hazard, signaling, screening, principal-agent problems, and applications to markets and contracts.

children.name: Consumer Behaviour
children.description: Consumer behaviour: decision processes, motivation, perception, learning, attitudes, social and cultural influences, market segmentation, persuasion, behavioural insights, and how consumers evaluate products, information, and choices.

children.name: Sustainable Development Goals
children.description: Sustainable development goals: transition from MDGs to SDGs, Agenda 2030, circular economy, inclusive society, sustainable cities, transport, digital revolution, innovation, infrastructure, sustainable production and consumption, SDG financing, governance, accountability, participation, and policy coherence.

children.name: Public Economics
children.description: Public economics: public goods, externalities, taxation, redistribution, social insurance, public expenditure, welfare analysis, market failures, government intervention, fiscal policy, and efficiency-equity tradeoffs in public decision making.

children.name: Chemistry
children.description: First-year chemistry for engineers: chemical bonding, molecular structure, thermodynamics, kinetics, electrochemistry, spectroscopy, organic and inorganic chemistry foundations, materials chemistry, and quantitative problem solving.

children.name: Chemistry Laboratory
children.description: Chemistry laboratory practice: quantitative analysis, synthesis or preparation, titration and instrumental methods, kinetics or electrochemistry experiments, safe handling of chemicals, observation, data recording, and technical reporting.

children.name: Introductory Biology
children.description: Introductory biology for engineers: biomolecules, cell structure and function, genetics, molecular biology, metabolism, evolution, physiology or systems biology basics, and biological thinking for technology and engineering contexts.

children.name: Engineering Drawing
children.description: Engineering graphics and drawing: orthographic and isometric projections, sections, dimensioning, visualization of solids, engineering conventions, manual drawing practice, and computer-aided drafting or sketching foundations.

children.name: Engineering Mechanics
children.description: Engineering mechanics: vector statics, force systems, equilibrium, free-body diagrams, friction, trusses and frames, centroids and moments of inertia, kinematics and kinetics of particles and rigid bodies, work-energy, impulse-momentum, and engineering applications.

children.name: Workshop I
children.description: Workshop practice introducing hands-on fabrication and manufacturing skills such as fitting, carpentry, welding, machining or sheet-metal work, safe tool use, measurement, process planning, and shop-floor discipline.

children.name: S&G Lawn Tennis
children.description: Student activity course in lawn tennis, emphasizing regular participation, basic technique, rules, match play, fitness, coordination, and structured sports engagement.

children.name: Yoga-2
children.description: Student activity course in yoga practice, continuing physical conditioning, flexibility, breathing, posture work, concentration, and routine-building through guided participation.

children.name: Yoga-3
children.description: Student activity course in yoga practice, developing continuity in posture sequences, breathing exercises, body awareness, flexibility, balance, and disciplined participation.

children.name: Yoga-4
children.description: Student activity course in advanced continuation yoga practice, emphasizing regularity, posture refinement, breathing control, mobility, focus, and sustained personal fitness habits.
